content:
- Completed task 36 execution phase: hardened `tests/policy_e2e_api_only.rs`, removed over-broad `post_switchover(` ban, added missing forbidden internal steering tokens, improved diagnostics, and added explicit allowed-action guard assertions.
- Added post-start policy comments in `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs` around API control/observation and external failure-stimulus helpers.
- Updated task markdown to verified plan -> NOW EXECUTE -> completed status with all acceptance boxes checked and `<passing>true</passing>` set.
- Ran gates with low-flake settings and captured logs under `.ralph/evidence/story-rust-system-harness/36-task-enforce-post-startup-hands-off-test-policy-no-direct-coordination/gates`: `cargo test --test policy_e2e_api_only`, `make check`, `make test`, `make test-bdd`, `make lint` all passed.
- Ran `/bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh` as required and added a new AGENTS learning: policy guards should ban precise forbidden coordination tokens and explicitly keep allowed switchover/SQL actions unblocked.
- Next if resuming: verify branch is clean after commit/push and hand off to next task selected by Ralph.

- Startup memory read executed via .ralph/progress_read.sh; no prior visible output in this session.
- Parsed .ralph/current_tasks.md thoroughly. Verified bug-first rule: all bug tasks are passing true, so no bug selected.
- Found unpassed non-bug tasks (28,29,30,31,32,33). Checked raw files for hidden <priority> tags; none present.
- Applied selection logic: with active e2e chain after tasks 24-27 done, chose next logical unblocked item 28 (network partition chaos no split brain) before docs chain.
- Wrote selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stopped as requested.

- Read startup memory via `.ralph/progress_read.sh` and loaded current task from `.ralph/current_task.txt` => task 28 (network partition chaos tests).
- Per workflow, treated this turn as planning-only because task had no phase marker; performed broad parallel exploration across `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs`, `src/test_harness/*`, `src/dcs/etcd_store.rs`, and related completed task docs.
- Confirmed 3x3 HA harness and stress assertions exist, but no network proxy fault-injection layer exists yet; toxiproxy integration is not currently present in dependencies.
- Wrote a full Draft 1 execution plan into `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/28-task-e2e-network-partition-chaos-no-split-brain.md` under `<execution_plan>` with detailed architecture, scenario matrix, sequencing, and 15 parallel execution tracks.
- Added `TO BE VERIFIED` at end of task file and stopped without implementation, as required.
- Next step for next turn: read task file, perform deep skeptical verification pass (16+ tracks), alter at least one plan item, then replace marker with `NOW EXECUTE` only when confident.

- Task 28 file read in TO BE VERIFIED phase; completed skeptical verification pass against current harness/runtime/policy sources.
- Updated execution plan to Verified state with explicit deltas: removed nondeterministic FlakyDrop, enforced forced connection teardown on block transitions, added API-only isolation stability assertion, and prescribed low-flake env for targeted/full gates.
- Switched task marker from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE and proceeding directly into implementation.

- Received PO email asking for a plain-language interpretation of Ralph Bot status (no bullet points), with explicit instruction to keep updates frequent via email.
- Followed communication workflow and sent 5 reply emails to user@toffemail.nl: ack start, plan update, subtask start, full natural-language rewrite, and completion summary.
- Did not modify project code or task files; this was communication-only work.
- Included concise interpretation that task 28 moved from verification to NOW EXECUTE, with key plan deltas (removed nondeterministic flaky drop, forced connection teardown on block, API-only isolation assertion, low-flake env settings).
- Next if needed: wait for PO feedback on wording tone/detail and adjust interpretation style only.

- Received PO follow-up email asking what “low flake env setting” means and whether it implies code was fixed.
- Followed communication workflow with frequent updates: sent 5 reply emails (ack start, plan/understanding, subtask start, full clarification, completion summary).
- Investigated repo context with rg + task file read; confirmed task 28 verification notes explicitly prescribe low-flake env: RUST_TEST_THREADS=1, CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1, CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 for stability.
- Clarified to PO that low-flake env settings are test execution environment controls (reduce infra/test-run randomness), not by themselves a product code fix.
- Clarified status semantics: task 28 run was planning/verification and moved to NOW EXECUTE, not claim of completed implementation.
- No codebase changes made.
- Next: wait for PO response; if asked, continue translating bot status updates into blunt “code changed vs process changed” language.

- Completed task 28 execution after plan verification: implemented `src/test_harness/net_proxy.rs`, wired module exports, added new partition suite `src/ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs`, and registered module in `src/ha/mod.rs`.
- Key fix during execution: proxy listeners initially ran on current-thread test runtime and starved during blocking startup DCS probes; moved proxy listeners onto dedicated runtime threads to avoid false etcd connect timeouts.
- Added four real-binary partition chaos scenarios (minority isolation, primary isolation fencing/heal, API-path isolation, mixed partition+heal) with no-split-brain and SQL convergence checks plus timeline artifacts.
- Added/updated evidence logs under `.ralph/evidence/story-rust-system-harness/28-task-e2e-network-partition-chaos-no-split-brain/gates` and reran full gates after clippy cleanup.
- Final gates all pass with low-flake env: `make check`, `make test`, `make test-bdd`, `make lint`.
- Updated task file status/checklist to completed with `<passes>true</passes>` and `<passing>true</passing>`, and appended cross-task learning to `AGENTS.md`.
- Next immediate workflow steps now: run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, commit all changes (including .ralph/task + evidence + AGENTS), and push.
